question: Which morning is most you?

options.label: Chaos, but cozy
options.imagePrompt: Overhead shot of a rumpled cream linen bed with a half-drunk lavender latte, phone face-down, and a half-open planner with no writing, soft morning light, pastel blush and cream tones, no people

options.label: Lists everywhere
options.imagePrompt: A sage green desk covered in overlapping sticky notes, a barely touched breakfast plate, and three open notebooks, warm diffused light, pastel sage and cream tones, no people

options.label: Already spiraling
options.imagePrompt: A bathroom counter with toothpaste uncapped, keys dropped in the sink, a forgotten coffee mug steaming, cool lavender morning light, pastel lavender and white tones, no people

options.label: Fake productive
options.imagePrompt: A neatly arranged desk with a glowing laptop open to a blank document, color-coded pens untouched, a to-do list with zero items crossed off, warm blush and cream light, no people

overlayText: Your pick reveals more than you think
caption: Tap the one that hurts a little. Then take the quiz.

question: Pick the desk that is you

options.label: Organized chaos
options.imagePrompt: A wide wooden desk buried in layered papers, open books, sticky notes, snack wrappers, and three half-finished projects, warm afternoon light, cream and blush tones, no people

options.label: Suspiciously bare
options.imagePrompt: A completely empty minimalist sage green desk with one closed notebook and a single pen, soft cool light, everything too tidy, pastel sage and white tones, no people

options.label: Coffee ring graveyard
options.imagePrompt: A light wood desk covered in overlapping coffee mug rings, a forgotten mug, crumpled notes, and a charger tangled around nothing, warm lavender-tinted light, blush and cream tones, no people

options.label: Fifteen tabs open
options.imagePrompt: A laptop on a messy pastel blush desk with a browser showing many tiny tabs, notebooks open to different pages, headphones half on the keyboard, soft warm glow, no people

overlayText: One of these knows your ADHD type
caption: Pick yours. The quiz breaks down what it means.

question: How does your brain feel at 3pm?

options.label: Completely offline
options.imagePrompt: A half-eaten snack next to a closed laptop on a cream couch cushion, afternoon light casting long shadows, a blanket half pulled over, pastel lavender and cream tones, no people

options.label: Finally switched on
options.imagePrompt: A sunlit sage desk with a suddenly open notebook full of handwriting, a fresh coffee steaming, window light streaming in at a low afternoon angle, sage and blush tones, no people

options.label: Anxious but frozen
options.imagePrompt: A pastel blush desk with a glowing screen, a long to-do list visible, a pen held mid-air over blank paper, tension in the stillness, cool lavender light, no people

options.label: Hyperfocus tunnel
options.imagePrompt: A cluttered cream desk with a single bright lamp illuminating one focused work zone, everything else faded into soft darkness around it, warm golden tones, no people

overlayText: Your 3pm has a name. Find it.
caption: This is more specific to your type than you think.

question: Which planner is collecting dust?

options.label: The perfect one
options.imagePrompt: A beautifully designed pastel lavender bullet journal open to a perfectly drawn spread that is completely blank and unused, soft side light, cream and lavender tones, no people

options.label: The digital app
options.imagePrompt: A phone screen showing a colorful productivity app with zero tasks completed and a streak broken, resting on a blush pink surface, soft light, no people

options.label: The sticky note wall
options.imagePrompt: A wall covered in overlapping pastel sticky notes in blush, lavender, and sage, many peeling off, a few with urgent words, chaotic but colorful, warm diffused light, no people

options.label: The new notebook
options.imagePrompt: A pristine sage green hardcover notebook opened to the first page with only a date written and nothing else, a pen resting across it, soft morning light, cream and sage tones, no people

overlayText: The right system exists for your type
caption: Not all planners are built for all brains. Quiz link up.

question: Pick your version of a deadline

options.label: Paralyzed early
options.imagePrompt: A pastel cream desk with a calendar showing a deadline two weeks away, a blank open notebook, a full untouched coffee, and a frozen stillness, soft lavender morning light, no people

options.label: Alive at midnight
options.imagePrompt: A dimly lit blush pink desk at night with a glowing laptop, empty snack wrappers, scribbled notes everywhere, a half-finished energy drink, urgent warm lamp glow, no people

overlayText: Both are ADHD. They are different types.
caption: Which one is you? Your type changes everything.

question: What does overstimulation look like for you?

options.label: Noise becomes pain
options.imagePrompt: A pair of pastel lavender noise-canceling headphones resting on a cream desk next to a closed window with blurred city light outside, soft cool tones, no people

options.label: Screen shutdown
options.imagePrompt: A laptop closed firmly on a sage green surface, phone flipped face-down beside it, a steaming cup of tea the only active thing, muted warm light, no people

options.label: Snapping at nothing
options.imagePrompt: A blush pink kitchen counter with a knocked-over pen cup, scattered papers, a chair pushed out abruptly, afternoon light harsh and flat, pastel blush and cream tones, no people

options.label: Blanket escape
options.imagePrompt: A rumpled cream and lavender bed with a person-shaped lump hidden entirely under soft blankets, curtains drawn, a phone turned off on the nightstand, muted lavender light, no people

overlayText: Your pattern points to your ADHD type
caption: Overstimulation shows up differently depending on your type.

question: Pick the task you avoid most

options.label: Replying to texts
options.imagePrompt: A phone resting on a blush pink surface with a screen showing a long list of unread message notifications, untouched, soft warm light, no people

options.label: Starting anything
options.imagePrompt: A sage green desk with a blank open notebook, a pen laid across the first line but nothing written, a full coffee going cold, still morning light, no people

options.label: Admin and forms
options.imagePrompt: A small pile of unopened pastel envelopes and an unfinished form on a cream desk, a pen nearby but capped, late afternoon lavender-tinted light, no people

options.label: Finishing things
options.imagePrompt: A desk with four different projects each 90 percent complete, notebooks open to near-final pages, tabs open, all slightly abandoned, warm blush and cream tones, no people

overlayText: Avoidance has a root cause. Find yours.
caption: The thing you dodge most is a clue to your type.

overlayText: ADHD brains aren't lazy — they're waiting for a dopamine reason to start.
caption: It's not willpower. It's brain chemistry.
imagePrompt: Cozy minimal flat lay on a cream linen surface, a single small potted succulent, soft morning window light casting gentle shadows, lavender and sage tones, pastel aesthetic, no text, no people, warm and calm mood

overlayText: Rejection Sensitive Dysphoria is real — ADHD makes criticism feel physically painful.
caption: Your reaction isn't dramatic. It's neurological.
imagePrompt: Soft close-up of a pale blush pillow on a cream bed with a single dried flower resting on it, diffused natural light, muted pastel tones, cozy and quiet mood, no text, no people

overlayText: Time blindness isn't rudeness. ADHD brains experience time as 'now' or 'not now'.
caption: The clock means something different to us.
imagePrompt: Minimal sage green desk corner with a small empty ceramic cup and soft afternoon light streaming across the surface, pastel and airy, cozy still-life, no text, no people, no clock imagery

overlayText: ADHD hyperfocus isn't a superpower — it's your brain locking in without your permission.
caption: Control is the part that's missing.
imagePrompt: Close-up of an open blank journal on a lavender linen surface, a single pen resting on the page, soft warm light, calm and introspective mood, pastel aesthetic, no text, no people

overlayText: Low dopamine makes starting tasks feel like lifting a thousand pounds.
caption: That wall is real. You're not making it up.

overlayText: ADHD working memory is like a browser with too many tabs — and no save button.
